rationale_for_baseline_tier: The underlying diagnosis is benign acute mechanical lumbar strain with a NEGATIVE red-flag screen (no cauda-equina, radicular, fracture or infective features on history), which in isolation would be T1-T2 self-care plus GP review. The tier is uplifted to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment) for two narrow, legitimate reasons: (1) the patient is completely incapacitated on the floor, cannot bear weight and cannot self-transport, and (2) telehealth cannot perform the palpation, straight-leg-raise and neurological examination that formally exclude a structural/cord cause — a plausible serious mimic that would need urgent in-person management if present. It is NOT T4/T5: with a negative red-flag screen a benign strain does not warrant an emergency-ambulance/000 response, though an ambulance may be used purely for extraction if no other means of in-person assessment exists.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New numbness around the groin/saddle area, or any loss of bladder or bowel control (or new inability to pass urine), or new weakness in both legs
escalation_edges.rationale: These are cauda-equina features — a time-critical surgical emergency requiring immediate emergency assessment and imaging.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you notice numbness around your groin or bottom, lose control of your bladder or bowels, can't pass urine, or both legs go weak — call 000 straight away and say it may be a spinal cord/nerve emergency.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New shooting pain, numbness or pins-and-needles travelling down one or both legs, or new leg weakness
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day urgent review if it develops
escalation_edges.rationale: Emerging radicular symptoms suggest nerve-root involvement (disc herniation) and warrant prompt in-person reassessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If pain, numbness or tingling starts shooting down your leg, or your leg feels weak, get seen urgently the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, night pain that wakes you and is not relieved by rest, or unexplained weight loss
escalation_edges.rationale: These features raise concern for infection or malignancy masquerading as mechanical back pain and need timely assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a fever, back pain that wakes you at night and won't ease with rest, or you're losing weight without trying, see a doctor promptly.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to be safely assessed or moved in person by any other means while incapacitated on the floor
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: If no home-visit/on-site urgent review can attend
escalation_edges.rationale: If the patient cannot otherwise be examined or extracted, ambulance transport to an emergency/urgent-care setting provides assessment, analgesia and safe mobilisation — used for logistics/incapacity, not because the strain is life-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If we can't get someone to you at home or on-site, we'll arrange an ambulance to help move you safely and get you assessed — not because it's life-threatening, but because you can't get up on your own.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Stranded on the floor of the workplace, unable to bear weight or move to a vehicle
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Remote self-transport to a GP or ED is not feasible — arrange in-person attendance (home/on-site urgent visit) or ambulance extraction; do not advise the patient to get up and drive.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: A facility manager is on scene and can assist and keep the patient safe and still
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use the on-scene person to keep the patient comfortable and immobile, unlock access for responders, and relay any change in symptoms.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Occupational injury requiring formal documentation and a workplace-injury claim
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure a medical certificate and workplace-injury documentation are provided as part of follow-up, and safe return-to-work planning is arranged.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a severe muscle injury to your lower back, and the good news is your description doesn't point to nerve damage. But you can't get up and you can't be examined over video, so you need to be seen in person urgently today — stay lying still, don't try to stand or force any movement, and we'll arrange someone to come to you or transport to help. Take simple pain relief only if you can safely reach and swallow it.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person assessment arranged today (home/on-site urgent visit, or ambulance for extraction if needed); call 000 immediately if the emergency features below appear.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours and no urgent in-person review is available, use an ambulance for assessment and safe mobilisation; 000 is the immediate path if any cauda-equina red flag appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document as a workplace manual-handling injury; provide a medical certificate and ensure GP review in ~3 days for functional recovery and return-to-work planning.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as routine self-care/GP back pain and managing it remotely — the patient is floor-bound and cannot be examined to exclude a structural/cord cause.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: No in-person review and no cauda-equina safety-net for an incapacitated, un-examined patient.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A missed early cauda-equina/structural cause progresses without examination, or the patient is left immobile and unmanaged on the floor.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Blanket 000 emergency-ambulance activation for a benign mechanical strain with a negative red-flag screen (ambulance for extraction is acceptable, but framed as logistics, not a life threat).

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: A structural/neurological cause (disc herniation, fracture, early cauda equina) cannot be examination-excluded via telehealth, and the patient cannot mobilise.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Screen red flags by history, arrange urgent in-person assessment that can examine and safely mobilise, and give a conservative 000/ED safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure remotely and advise home self-care, or over-react with a blanket emergency-tier response despite a negative red-flag screen.
